premises.content: DMDP passivation leads to a fivefold longer carrier lifetime and one-third the photoluminescence quantum yield loss compared to control
premises.label: carrier_lifetime_5x

premises.content: DMDP-treated devices show a dark saturation current (J0) reduction by two orders of magnitude compared with control devices
premises.label: dark_current_reduction

premises.content: Diammonium ligands (PDAI2) induce n-type doping and a surface dipole that repels minority carriers (holes) from the perovskite/C60 interface, achieving field-effect passivation that reduces contact-induced interface recombination
premises.label: field_effect_passivation

premises.content: The sulfur-modified methylthio molecule (3MTPA) has stronger binding energy to iodide vacancy defects on the perovskite surface compared to conventional alkyl ammonium ligands, driven by S-Pb coordination bonding and enhanced hydrogen bonding with formamidinium
premises.label: methylthio_binding

premises.content: The DMDP strategy improves PCE by 13-14% for both narrow-bandgap (~1.2 eV) and wide-bandgap (~1.8 eV) perovskite compositions, demonstrating universality across perovskite bandgaps
premises.label: universal_bandgap
